title: CI: parallel-safe userns test
body:
- use safename
- add ci:parallel tags where possible
  - where not possible, document why

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: I had a foolish thought, that although it might not be parallel-safe to run _multiple_ `userns=auto` tests, it might be OK to run _one_ in parallel with all the other userns tests. But no: failure on my first try. This suggests to me that a rootless user is limited, can cannot run a `userns=auto` container while another container is running? I have to be missing something.

> I had a foolish thought, that although it might not be parallel-safe to run _multiple_ `userns=auto` tests, it might be OK to run _one_ in parallel with all the other userns tests. But no: failure on my first try. This suggests to me that a rootless user is limited, can cannot run a `userns=auto` container while another container is running? I have to be missing something.

Correct because another running container by default eats up all uids in the storage and auto really want a range that is not used already.
